member.name: T:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.LazyLoadingProxiesOptionsBuilder
member.summary: Allows SQL Server specific configuration to be performed on .
member.remarks: Instances of this class are returned from a call to and it is not designed to be directly constructed in your application code.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.LazyLoadingProxiesOptionsBuilder.#ctor(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The core options builder.

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.LazyLoadingProxiesOptionsBuilder.OptionsBuilder
member.summary: Gets the core options builder.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.LazyLoadingProxiesOptionsBuilder.IgnoreNonVirtualNavigations(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Configures the proxies to ignore navigations that are not virtual. By default, an exception will be thrown if a non-virtual navigation is found.
member.param: to ignore navigations that are not virtual. The default value is , meaning an exception will be thrown if a non-virtual navigation is found.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.LazyLoadingProxiesOptionsBuilder.WithOption(System.Func{Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Proxies.Internal.ProxiesOptionsExtension,Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Proxies.Internal.ProxiesOptionsExtension})
member.summary: Sets an option by cloning the extension used to store the settings. This ensures the builder does not modify options that are already in use elsewhere.
member.param: An action to set the option.
member.returns: The same builder instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: T:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.ProxiesStrings
member.summary: This is an internal API that supports the Entity Framework Core infrastructure and not subject to the same compatibility standards as public APIs. It may be changed or removed without notice in any release. You should only use it directly in your code with extreme caution and knowing that doing so can result in application failures when updating to a new Entity Framework Core release.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.ProxiesStrings.DictionaryCannotBeProxied(System.Object,System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: The type '{dictionaryType}' used for shared entity type '{entityType}' is not suitable for use as a change-tracking proxy because its indexer property is not virtual. Consider using an implementation of '{interfaceType}' that allows overriding of the indexer.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.ProxiesStrings.EntityTypeNotFoundShared(System.Object)
member.summary: The type '{clrType}' is configured as a shared-type entity type, but the entity type name is not known. Ensure that CreateProxy is called on a DbSet created specifically for the shared-type entity type through use of a 'DbContext.Set' overload that accepts an entity type name.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.ProxiesStrings.FieldProperty(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Property '{property}' on entity type '{entityType}' is mapped without a CLR property. 'UseChangeTrackingProxies' requires all entity types to be public, unsealed, have virtual properties, and have a public or protected constructor. 'UseLazyLoadingProxies' requires only the navigation properties be virtual.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.ProxiesStrings.ItsASeal(System.Object)
member.summary: Entity type '{entityType}' is sealed. 'UseChangeTrackingProxies' requires all entity types to be public, unsealed, have virtual properties, and have a public or protected constructor. 'UseLazyLoadingProxies' requires only the navigation properties be virtual.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.ProxiesStrings.NonVirtualIndexerProperty(System.Object)
member.summary: The mapped indexer property on entity type '{entityType}' is not virtual. 'UseChangeTrackingProxies' requires all entity types to be public, unsealed, have virtual properties, and have a public or protected constructor. 'UseLazyLoadingProxies' requires only the navigation properties be virtual.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.ProxiesStrings.NonVirtualProperty(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Property '{1_entityType}.{0_property}' is not virtual. 'UseChangeTrackingProxies' requires all entity types to be public, unsealed, have virtual properties, and have a public or protected constructor. 'UseLazyLoadingProxies' requires only the navigation properties be virtual.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.ProxiesStrings.ProxiesNotEnabled(System.Object)
member.summary: Unable to create proxy for '{entityType}' because proxies are not enabled. Call 'DbContextOptionsBuilder.UseChangeTrackingProxies' or 'DbContextOptionsBuilder.UseLazyLoadingProxies' to enable proxies.

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Internal.ProxiesStrings.ProxyServicesMissing
member.summary: 'UseChangeTrackingProxies' and 'UseLazyLoadingProxies' each require 'AddEntityFrameworkProxies' to be called on the internal service provider used.

member.name: T:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods related to use of proxies with Entity Framework Core.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.UseChangeTrackingProxies(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Turns on the creation of change tracking proxies.

remarks.para: Note that this requires appropriate services to be available in the EF internal service provider. Normally this will happen automatically, but if the application is controlling the service provider, then a call to may be needed.
See Notification entities for more information and examples.

member.param: The options builder, as passed to or exposed AddDbContext.
to use change tracking proxies; to prevent their use.
if proxy change detection should check if the incoming value is equal to the current value before notifying. Defaults to .
member.returns: The same builder to allow method calls to be chained.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.UseChangeTrackingProxies``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder{``0},System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Turns on the creation of change tracking proxies.

remarks.para: Note that this requires appropriate services to be available in the EF internal service provider. Normally this will happen automatically, but if the application is controlling the service provider, then a call to may be needed.
See Notification entities for more information and examples.

member.param: The options builder, as passed to or exposed AddDbContext.
to use change tracking proxies; to prevent their use.
if proxy change detection should check if the incoming value is equal to the current value before notifying. Defaults to .
member.returns: The same builder to allow method calls to be chained.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.UseLazyLoadingProxies(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Turns on the creation of lazy loading proxies.

remarks.para: Note that this requires appropriate services to be available in the EF internal service provider. Normally this will happen automatically, but if the application is controlling the service provider, then a call to may be needed.
See Lazy loading for more information and examples.

member.param: The options builder, as passed to or exposed AddDbContext.
to use lazy loading proxies; to prevent their use.
member.returns: The same builder to allow method calls to be chained.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.UseLazyLoadingProxies(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder,System.Action{Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.LazyLoadingProxiesOptionsBuilder})
member.summary: Turns on the creation of lazy loading proxies.

remarks.para: Note that this requires appropriate services to be available in the EF internal service provider. Normally this will happen automatically, but if the application is controlling the service provider, then a call to may be needed.
See Lazy loading for more information and examples.

member.param: The options builder, as passed to or exposed AddDbContext.
An optional action to allow additional proxy-specific configuration.
member.returns: The same builder to allow method calls to be chained.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.UseLazyLoadingProxies``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder{``0},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Turns on the creation of lazy loading proxies.

remarks.para: Note that this requires appropriate services to be available in the EF internal service provider. Normally this will happen automatically, but if the application is controlling the service provider, then a call to may be needed.
See Lazy loading for more information and examples.

member.param: The options builder, as passed to or exposed AddDbContext.
to use lazy loading proxies; to prevent their use.
member.returns: The same builder to allow method calls to be chained.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.UseLazyLoadingProxies``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContextOptionsBuilder{``0},System.Action{Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.LazyLoadingProxiesOptionsBuilder})
member.summary: Turns on the creation of lazy loading proxies.

remarks.para: Note that this requires appropriate services to be available in the EF internal service provider. Normally this will happen automatically, but if the application is controlling the service provider, then a call to may be needed.
See Lazy loading for more information and examples.

member.param: The options builder, as passed to or exposed AddDbContext.
An optional action to allow additional proxy-specific configuration.
member.returns: The same builder to allow method calls to be chained.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.CreateProxy(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Type,System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a proxy instance for an entity type if proxy creation has been turned on.
member.param: The .
The entity type for which a proxy is needed.
Arguments to pass to the entity type constructor.
member.returns: The proxy instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.CreateProxy``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a proxy instance for an entity type if proxy creation has been turned on.
member.typeparam: The entity type for which a proxy is needed.
member.param: The .
Arguments to pass to the entity type constructor.
member.returns: The proxy instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.CreateProxy``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbContext,System.Action{``0},System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a proxy instance for an entity type if proxy creation has been turned on.
member.typeparam: The entity type for which a proxy is needed.
member.param: The .
Called after the entity is created to set property values, etc.
Arguments to pass to the entity type constructor.
member.returns: The proxy instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.CreateProxy``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbSet{``0},System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a proxy instance for an entity type if proxy creation has been turned on.
member.typeparam: The entity type for which a proxy is needed.
member.param: The .
Arguments to pass to the entity type constructor.
member.returns: The proxy instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ProxiesExtensions.CreateProxy``1(Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.DbSet{``0},System.Action{``0},System.Object[])
member.summary: Creates a proxy instance for an entity type if proxy creation has been turned on.
member.typeparam: The entity type for which a proxy is needed.
member.param: The .
Called after the entity is created to set property values, etc.
Arguments to pass to the entity type constructor.
member.returns: The proxy instance.

member.name: T:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Proxies.Internal.IProxyFactory
member.summary: This is an internal API that supports the Entity Framework Core infrastructure and not subject to the same compatibility standards as public APIs. It may be changed or removed without notice in any release. You should only use it directly in your code with extreme caution and knowing that doing so can result in application failures when updating to a new Entity Framework Core release.

member.name: T:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.BidirectionalDictionary`2
member.summary: Represents a dictionary with non-null unique values that contains an inverse dictionary.
member.typeparam: The type of the keys in the dictionary.
The type of the values in the dictionary.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.BidirectionalDictionary`2.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class that is empty, has the default initial capacity, and uses the default equality comparers.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.BidirectionalDictionary`2.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class that is empty, has the specified initial capacity, and uses the default equality comparers.
member.param: The initial number of elements that can contain.

member.param: The implementation to use when comparing keys, or null to use the default for the type of the key.

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.BidirectionalDictionary`2.Item(`0)
member.summary: Gets or sets the value associated with the specified key.
member.param: The key of the value to get or set.
member.returns: The value associated with the specified key. If the specified key is not found, a get operation throws a , and a set operation creates a new element with the specified key.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.BidirectionalDictionary`2.Add(`0,`1)
member.summary: Adds the specified key and value to the dictionary.
member.param: The key of the element to add.
The value of the element to add.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.BidirectionalDictionary`2.Remove(`0)
member.summary: Removes the value with the specified key from the .
member.param: The key of the element to remove.
member.returns: if the element is successfully found and removed; otherwise, . This method returns if key is not found in the .

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.BidirectionalDictionary`2.Remove(`0,`1@)
member.summary: Removes the value with the specified key from the .
member.param: The key of the element to remove.

When this method returns, contains the value associated with the specified key, if the key is found; otherwise, the default value for the type of the value parameter. This parameter is passed uninitialized.
member.returns: if the element is successfully found and removed; otherwise, . This method returns if key is not found in the .

When this method returns, contains the value associated with the specified key, if the key is found; otherwise, the default value for the type of the value parameter. This parameter is passed uninitialized.
member.returns: if the contains an element with the specified key; otherwise, .

member.summary: Resizes the internal data structure if necessary to ensure no additional resizing to support the specified capacity.

member.returns: The capacity of the .

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.BidirectionalDictionary`2.TrimExcess
member.summary: Sets the capacity of an object to the actual number of elements it contains, rounded up to a nearby, implementation-specific value.

member.summary: Returns an wrapper for the current dictionary.
member.returns: An object that acts as a read-only wrapper around the current .

member.name: T:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2
member.summary: Represents an ordered collection of keys and values with the same performance as with O(1) lookups and adds but with O(n) inserts and removes.
member.typeparam: The type of the keys in the dictionary.
The type of the values in the dictionary.

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of key/value pairs contained in the .
member.returns: The number of key/value pairs contained in the .

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Comparer
member.summary: Gets the that is used to determine equality of keys for the dictionary.
member.returns: The generic interface implementation that is used to determine equality of keys for the current and to provide hash values for the keys.

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Keys
member.summary: Gets a collection containing the keys in the .
member.returns: An containing the keys in the .

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Values
member.summary: Gets a collection containing the values in the .
member.returns: An containing the values in the .

member.summary: Gets or sets the value associated with the specified key as an O(1) operation.
member.param: The key of the value to get or set.
member.returns: The value associated with the specified key. If the specified key is not found, a get operation throws a , and a set operation creates a new element with the specified key.
member.exception: is null.
The property is retrieved and does not exist in the collection.

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets or sets the value at the specified index as an O(1) operation.
member.param: The zero-based index of the element to get or set.
member.returns: The value at the specified index.
member.exception: is less than 0.-or- is equal to or greater than .

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.GetOrAdd(`0,`1)
member.summary: Adds a key/value pair to the if the key does not already exist as an O(1) operation.
member.param: The key of the element to add.
The value to be added, if the key does not already exist.
member.returns: The value for the key. This will be either the existing value for the key if the key is already in the dictionary, or the new value if the key was not in the dictionary.
member.exception: is null.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.GetOrAdd(`0,System.Func{`1})
member.summary: Adds a key/value pair to the by using the specified function, if the key does not already exist as an O(1) operation.
member.param: The key of the element to add.
The function used to generate a value for the key.
member.returns: The value for the key. This will be either the existing value for the key if the key is already in the dictionary, or the new value for the key as returned by valueFactory if the key was not in the dictionary.
member.exception: is null.-or- is null.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.IndexOf(`0)
member.summary: Returns the zero-based index of the element with the specified key within the as an O(1) operation.
member.param: The key of the element to locate.
member.returns: The zero-based index of the element with the specified key within the , if found; otherwise, -1.
member.exception: is null.

The key of the element to insert.
The value of the element to insert.
member.exception: is null.
An element with the same key already exists in the .
is less than 0.-or- is greater than .

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Insert(`0,`1)
member.summary: Inserts the element in this sorted dictionary to the corresponding index using the default comparer.
member.param: The key of the element to insert.
The value of the element to insert.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Insert(`0,`1,System.Collections.Generic.IComparer{`0})
member.summary: Inserts the element in this sorted dictionary to the corresponding index using the default comparer.
member.param: The key of the element to insert.
The value of the element to insert.
The comparer to use.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Move(System.Int32,System.Int32)

The zero-based index to move the elements to.
The number of elements to move.

member.summary: Removes the value with the specified key from the as an O(n) operation.
member.param: The key of the element to remove.
member.returns: true if the element is successfully found and removed; otherwise, false. This method returns false if is not found in the .
member.exception: is null.

When this method returns, contains the value associated with the specified key, if the key is found; otherwise, the default value for the type of the parameter. This parameter is passed uninitialized.
member.returns: true if the element is successfully found and removed; otherwise, false. This method returns false if is not found in the .
member.exception: is null.

member.param: The key of the element to add.

member.returns: true if the element was added to the ; false if the already contained an element with the specified key.

member.summary: Gets the element at the current position of the enumerator.
member.returns: The element in the at the current position of the enumerator.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Enumerator.Dispose
member.summary: Releases all resources used by the .

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.Enumerator.MoveNext
member.summary: Advances the enumerator to the next element of the .
member.returns: true if the enumerator was successfully advanced to the next element; false if the enumerator has passed the end of the collection.
member.exception: The collection was modified after the enumerator was created.

member.summary: Represents the collection of keys in a . This class cannot be inherited.

member.returns: The number of elements contained in the .

member.summary: Advances the enumerator to the next element of the .
member.returns: true if the enumerator was successfully advanced to the next element; false if the enumerator has passed the end of the collection.
member.exception: The collection was modified after the enumerator was created.

member.name: T:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.ValueCollection
member.summary: Represents the collection of values in a . This class cannot be inherited.

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.ValueCollection.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of elements contained in the .
member.returns: The number of elements contained in the .

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.ValueCollection.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the value at the specified index as an O(1) operation.
member.param: The zero-based index of the value to get.
member.returns: The value at the specified index.
member.exception: is less than 0.-or- is equal to or greater than .

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.ValueCollection.GetEnumerator
member.summary: Returns an enumerator that iterates through the .
member.returns: A for the .

member.name: T:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.ValueCollection.Enumerator
member.summary: Enumerates the elements of a .

member.name: P:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.ValueCollection.Enumerator.Current
member.summary: Gets the element at the current position of the enumerator.
member.returns: The element in the at the current position of the enumerator.

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.ValueCollection.Enumerator.Dispose
member.summary: Releases all resources used by the .

member.name: M:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Utilities.OrderedDictionary`2.ValueCollection.Enumerator.MoveNext
member.summary: Advances the enumerator to the next element of the .
member.returns: true if the enumerator was successfully advanced to the next element; false if the enumerator has passed the end of the collection.
member.exception: The collection was modified after the enumerator was created.

member.name: T:Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.EFDiagnostics
member.summary: Contains the IDs of diagnostics emitted by EF Core analyzers, [Experimental] and other mechanisms.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.ProxiesServiceCollectionExtensions
member.summary: EntityFrameworkCore.Proxies extension methods for .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.ProxiesServiceCollectionExtensions.AddEntityFrameworkProxies(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Adds the services required for proxy support in Entity Framework.
member.remarks: Calling this method is no longer necessary when building most applications, including those that use dependency injection in ASP.NET or elsewhere. It is only needed when building the internal service provider for use with the method. This is not recommend other than for some advanced scenarios.
member.param: The to add services to.
member.returns: The same service collection so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitorExtensions.Visit(System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitor,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Linq.Expressions.Expression})
member.summary: Dispatches the list of expressions to one of the more specialized visit methods in this class.
member.param: The expression visitor.
The expressions to visit.
member.returns: The modified expression list, if any of the elements were modified; otherwise, returns the original expression list.

member.name: M:System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitorExtensions.VisitAndConvert``1(System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitor,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{``0},System.String)
member.summary: Visits an expression, casting the result back to the original expression type.
member.param: The expression visitor.
The expression to visit.
The name of the calling method; used to report a better error message.
member.returns: The modified expression, if it or any subexpression was modified; otherwise, returns the original expression.
member.exception: The visit method for this node returned a different type.

member.name: M:System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitorExtensions.Visit``1(System.Linq.Expressions.ExpressionVisitor,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{``0},System.Func{``0,``0})
member.summary: Visits all nodes in the collection using a specified element visitor.
member.param: The expression visitor.
The nodes to visit.
A delegate that visits a single element, optionally replacing it with a new element.
member.returns: The modified node list, if any of the elements were modified; otherwise, returns the original node list.

member.name: M:System.SharedTypeExtensions.DisplayName(System.Type,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: This is an internal API that supports the Entity Framework Core infrastructure and not subject to the same compatibility standards as public APIs. It may be changed or removed without notice in any release. You should only use it directly in your code with extreme caution and knowing that doing so can result in application failures when updating to a new Entity Framework Core release.
